TSG License Manager Workflow Guide

Confidence Summary: Product identity, RC1 version, audiences, and environment notes are [verified] from repository documentation; this header has no procedural steps.

Product Name: TSG License Manager

Product Type: SaaS/software

Version/Date: v0.2.0-rc.1 / June 24, 2026

Primary End-User: Product customers, licensed users, organization members, and account owners

Primary Admin: Administrators, support leads, operations owners, release managers, and product owners

Environment Notes: GCP staging and production-style operations; Cloud Run, Cloud SQL for PostgreSQL, Secret Manager, Artifact Registry, product-specific customer portals, Stripe webhooks, SMTP email, and product client APIs.

Grounding Corpus Used: Repository guides under README.md, docs/products/tsg_license_manager/, docs/rc1/, docs/operations/, docs/security/, docs/integration/, and docs/production_hardening.md.

Purpose: Provide one operating workflow for customer-facing license use and administrator-facing configuration, governance, support, and release operations.

At-a-Glance

Confidence Summary: Capabilities in this section are [verified] from customer and administrator guides; no procedural steps are included here.

End-User track: what the customer can doAdmin track: what the administrator can do
Set up or reset a portal password.Provision and govern tenants, products, plans, modules, and entitlements.
Sign in through the correct product portal.Manage customer records, licenses, device activations, and organization/team data.
View license status, plan/edition, device limits, and active devices.Review product IDs, tenant IDs, key prefixes, portal hostnames, and status.
Activate a product on an allowed device.Retrieve and govern product API credentials and endpoint references.
Deactivate an old or unused device when permitted.Map Stripe prices to product plans and review billing/webhook outcomes.
Review downloads, updates, extensions, and documentation links when enabled.Review email settings, setup/reset delivery, and email event records.
Review billing or subscription status when applicable.Monitor readiness, runtime errors, auth events, billing events, client events, and release evidence.
Contact support with license/account details without sharing passwords, API keys, or full payment card details.Run backup, restore, rollback, smoke-test, security, and acceptance checks.

Setup / Onboarding Sequence

Confidence Summary: End-user setup is fully [verified] from the end-user guide; admin setup is mostly [verified] from admin, deployment, security, and hardening guides, with one [unknown — needs SME input] regulated-data classification step.

End-User Track

Precondition: You have a welcome email, setup email, or portal URL from TTI Solutions or your product administrator.

  1. [verified] Open the setup email from TTI Solutions or open the product portal URL provided by your product administrator.
  2. [verified] Select the password setup link.
  3. [verified] Confirm the page shows the expected product or customer portal context.
  4. [verified] Enter a new password and submit the form.
  5. [verified] Return to the product portal.
  6. [verified] Sign in with your email address and new password.
  7. [verified] Open the license area or dashboard.
  8. [verified] Confirm the license shows the expected product, plan or edition, status, device limit, and active devices.
  9. [verified] Install and open the licensed product application when activation is required.
  10. [verified] Enter the product key in the product activation or license option.
  11. [verified] Submit activation and wait for confirmation that activation succeeded.

Expected result: The portal account is usable, the assigned license is visible, and the product activates on an allowed device.

How to confirm success: Confirm the portal dashboard loads, the license status is active or otherwise expected, the active device count is correct, and the product reports successful activation.

Admin Track

Precondition: You have approved administrative access, target environment details, and a staging or production deployment plan.

  1. [verified] Store DATABASE_URL, TSG_AUTH_SECRET, TSG_LICENSE_SIGNING_SECRET, TSG_CLIENT_API_SECRET, STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET, and SMTP credentials in Secret Manager or an approved secret store.
  2. [verified] Configure staging or production to use PostgreSQL, HTTPS, secure cookies, explicit allowed hosts, and non-placeholder secrets.
  3. [verified] Deploy the service to the approved runtime target, such as Cloud Run with Cloud SQL for PostgreSQL and Artifact Registry.
  4. [verified] Record the image digest, Cloud Run revision, database backup identifier, migration version, and /ready response.
  5. [verified] Sign in with an approved named admin account.
  6. [verified] Confirm the environment banner or base URL is the intended staging or production system.
  7. [verified] Verify tenant ID, display name, domain or portal hostname, theme, visibility group, and support email.
  8. [verified] Verify product ID, product name, tenant ID, product key prefix, and product status.
  9. [verified] Configure or validate plans, modules, entitlements, roles, and access boundaries before allowing customer activation.
  10. [unknown — needs SME input] Classify whether customer, license, billing, or activation records include CUI or other regulated data before configuring retention, export, and support-handling rules.

Expected result: The service is deployed, secure-by-configuration, tenant-aware, product-aware, and ready for controlled customer onboarding.

How to confirm success: /ready reports database OK, product portals route to the correct tenant/product context, admin seed credentials are absent from production, and release evidence records the deployment state.

Core Operating Procedures

Confidence Summary: Customer portal and admin procedures are primarily [verified] from the product guides, runbook, deployment guide, endpoint reference, and backup/restore guide; limited operational routing choices are [inferred — confirm] where the repository names the condition but not the final business owner.

End-User Track

Task EU-1: Sign In and Review a License

Trigger/Precondition: You need to confirm license status, plan, device limit, billing state, downloads, or team information.

  1. [verified] Open the product portal URL provided by TTI Solutions, your welcome email, or your product administrator.
  2. [verified] Enter the email address associated with your product license.
  3. [verified] Enter your password and select Sign In.
  4. [verified] Wait for the portal dashboard to load.
  5. [verified] Open the license area or dashboard.
  6. [verified] Review product name, product key, plan or edition, license status, device limit, active devices, timestamps, and billing or subscription status when shown.

Expected result: The portal shows the license and account details available to your role.

How to confirm success: The license belongs to the expected product and account, the status is clear, and the device count matches your known active devices.

Task EU-2: Activate a Product on a Device

Trigger/Precondition: The product is installed, the device is online, and you have a product key for the correct product.

  1. [verified] Install and open the product.
  2. [verified] Choose the activation or license option.
  3. [verified] Enter your product key.
  4. [verified] Confirm the device label if prompted.
  5. [verified] Submit activation.
  6. [verified] Wait for confirmation that activation succeeded.
  7. [verified] Restart the product if the app asks you to.

Expected result: TSG License Manager verifies that the key exists, the license is active, the license belongs to the correct product, the device limit is not exceeded, and the current device is allowed.

How to confirm success: The product reports successful activation, the portal shows the active device when device details are available, and licensed features load as expected.

Task EU-3: Deactivate an Old Device

Trigger/Precondition: You reached a device limit, replaced a workstation, or no longer need access on an old device.

  1. [verified] Sign in to the product portal.
  2. [verified] Open the license or device area.
  3. [verified] Review device names, IDs, or labels.
  4. [verified] Identify the old or unused device.
  5. [verified] Confirm you no longer need product access on that device.
  6. [verified] Select the deactivation action and confirm the action.
  7. [verified] Activate the product on the new device if needed.

Expected result: The old device no longer consumes an active device slot, and product access may be removed from that workstation immediately.

How to confirm success: The active device count changes as expected and the new device can activate if the license permits it.

Task EU-4: Reset a Password

Trigger/Precondition: You forgot your password, the setup link expired, or support asked you to complete a reset.

  1. [verified] Open the product portal.
  2. [verified] Select the password reset option when available.
  3. [verified] Enter your account email address.
  4. [verified] Open the reset email.
  5. [verified] Select the reset link.
  6. [verified] Enter a new password.
  7. [verified] Sign in again.

Expected result: The old password is replaced, and the account can sign in through the correct product portal.

How to confirm success: The portal accepts the new password and the dashboard loads.

Task EU-5: Resolve Missing Access, Downloads, or Extensions

Trigger/Precondition: A license, feature, download, product update, module, extension, invoice, or subscription detail is missing or appears incorrect.

  1. [verified] Confirm you are signed in with the email address tied to the license.
  2. [verified] Confirm you are on the correct product portal.
  3. [verified] Check license status, plan or edition, device limit, billing status, downloads, and extensions available to your account.
  4. [verified] Refresh license status from inside the product if that option is available.
  5. [verified] Contact support with product name, account email, app version, operating system, device label, partial product key details, screenshot or exact error message, and approximate time of the issue.
  6. [verified] Do not send passwords, full payment card details, API keys, private credentials, or configuration secrets to support.

Expected result: You either resolve the mismatch or provide support with enough information to inspect the account and license safely.

How to confirm success: The expected feature, download, license, billing record, or support response is visible without exposing secrets.

Admin Track

Task AD-1: Run the Daily Administrative Check

Trigger/Precondition: Start of an administrative session, smoke test, release check, or support triage window.

  1. [verified] Sign in with an approved admin account.
  2. [verified] Confirm the environment banner or base URL is the expected staging or production system.
  3. [verified] Check /ready or the readiness card for database connectivity.
  4. [verified] Review recent runtime errors.
  5. [verified] Review recent auth, billing, Stripe, email, and client events.
  6. [verified] Confirm no unexpected product or tenant records were created.
  7. [verified] Record any unusual finding in the release or operations log.

Expected result: The administrator knows whether the service is healthy and whether recent events require triage.

How to confirm success: /ready is healthy, monitoring pages show no unreviewed critical errors, and unusual findings are recorded.

Task AD-2: Validate Product, Plan, Module, and Marketplace Setup

Trigger/Precondition: A product is being added, reviewed for release, or audited after configuration changes.

  1. [verified] Confirm tenant ID, display name, domain or portal hostname, theme or customer-facing presentation, visibility group, and support email.
  2. [verified] Confirm product ID is stable and API-safe.
  3. [verified] Confirm product name is customer-facing.
  4. [verified] Confirm tenant ID points to the correct tenant.
  5. [verified] Confirm product key prefix is correct.
  6. [verified] Set product status to active only when the product is ready.
  7. [verified] Confirm each plan has plan ID, product ID, edition or name, billing type, device limit, status, and optional license duration.
  8. [verified] Do not activate paid plans until billing and Stripe mapping are validated.
  9. [verified] Map modules to plans so licenses receive expected access.
  10. [verified] Confirm the product application knows how to enforce each entitlement.
  11. [verified] Confirm marketplace extension ID, product ID, status, required plan, download URL, docs URL, and auditable install records when extensions are used.

Expected result: Product identity, portal context, billing plan, entitlements, and optional extensions are coherent before customer use.

How to confirm success: Product portal routing, license visibility, activation, refresh, downloads, and extension access match the intended product and plan.

Task AD-3: Support a Customer License or Device Issue

Trigger/Precondition: A customer reports missing license access, activation denial, device-limit failure, expired setup/reset link, or suspected account mismatch.

  1. [verified] Verify the customer's identity through the approved support procedure.
  2. [verified] Confirm the customer email is tied to the expected customer or license record.
  3. [verified] Open the relevant customer, license, or activation record.
  4. [verified] Review license product ID, plan ID, product key, status, device limit, and activation timestamps.
  5. [verified] Send or regenerate a setup/reset token only for the verified customer.
  6. [verified] Deactivate old devices only when requested or approved.
  7. [verified] Confirm device limit enforcement after changes.
  8. [verified] Do not ask customers to send passwords, full payment card details, API keys, private credentials, or configuration secrets.

Expected result: The customer receives a safe support action without breaking tenant boundaries or exposing secrets.

How to confirm success: The customer signs in or activates successfully, and the admin record shows the intended license/device state.

Task AD-4: Validate Stripe and Billing Operations

Trigger/Precondition: Paid products, subscriptions, invoices, Stripe webhooks, or billing-driven access changes are being configured or reviewed.

  1. [verified] Configure the Stripe test-mode webhook.
  2. [verified] Map each Stripe price as stripe_price_id -> product_id -> plan_id.
  3. [verified] Send a signed checkout event.
  4. [verified] Confirm signature verification succeeds.
  5. [verified] Confirm license and customer records are created or updated as expected.
  6. [verified] Confirm replayed events are idempotent.
  7. [verified] Confirm tampered payloads are rejected.
  8. [verified] Review subscription status, invoice status, failed payment events, billing-caused license status changes, and audit records.

Expected result: Billing events map to the right product and plan, and unsafe webhook payloads do not change customer access.

How to confirm success: Billing events, invoices, license status, Stripe logs, and audit records show the expected outcome for signed, replayed, and tampered events.

Task AD-5: Validate Email Operations

Trigger/Precondition: Password setup, password reset, license notices, or operational test messages must be accepted for staging or production.

  1. [verified] Confirm SMTP or provider credentials are stored in the environment or secret store.
  2. [verified] Confirm staging or production does not depend on database-stored SMTP passwords.
  3. [verified] Send a password setup email.
  4. [verified] Send a password reset email.
  5. [verified] Confirm delivery event records.
  6. [verified] Confirm email links point to the correct product portal or base URL.

Expected result: Account emails reach customers and route them to the correct product context.

How to confirm success: Email event records exist, delivery is successful, and links open the intended portal.

Task AD-6: Run Backup, Restore, and Rollback Validation

Trigger/Precondition: Before deployment, before promotion, during rollback rehearsal, or after restoring a database backup.

  1. [verified] Record the active image digest.
  2. [verified] Record the active Cloud Run revision.
  3. [verified] Record the active Cloud SQL backup identifier.
  4. [verified] Record the migration version.
  5. [verified] Record the /ready response.
  6. [verified] Run the backup script for the operator platform: scripts/backup_postgres.ps1 on PowerShell or scripts/backup_postgres.sh on Linux/macOS.
  7. [verified] Restore with scripts/restore_postgres.ps1 -BackupFile backups/tsg_platform_YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS.dump on PowerShell or scripts/restore_postgres.sh backups/tsg_platform_YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS.dump on Linux/macOS when restore is required.
  8. [verified] After restore, verify login, licenses, billing events, email outbox, and /ready.
  9. [verified] Re-run /health, /ready, portal login, tenant isolation, activation, and Stripe replay smoke tests after rollback.
  10. [verified] Record operators, timestamps, backup IDs, image digests, and validation results.

Expected result: Backup and rollback evidence proves that the service can be recovered and validated.

How to confirm success: Restored or rolled-back environments pass login, tenant isolation, activation, billing replay, email outbox, and readiness checks.

Admin Configuration & Governance

Confidence Summary: Configuration controls are [verified] from admin, deployment, security, hardening, and operations docs except regulated/CUI handling, which is [unknown — needs SME input] because no product-specific CUI handling rule appears in the grounding corpus.

Governance Control Map

Control areaAdmin actionEnd-user impactRisk flagBasis
Roles and permissionsUse named admin accounts; restrict admin console to approved users; apply least privilege for GCP permissions.Customers see only permitted account/license actions.Access control[verified]
Tenant and portal boundariesVerify tenant ID, portal hostname, product ID, key prefix, and status.Customers land in the correct product context.Access control[verified]
Product client API credentialsRetrieve product API settings through Product Integration Settings; authenticate with X-TSG-Product-ID and X-TSG-API-Key.Products can activate, refresh, deactivate, check updates, downloads, and extensions.Access control[verified]
SecretsStore database, auth, signing, client API, Stripe, and SMTP secrets outside source control.Reduces credential exposure risk.Access control[verified]
Stripe mappingVerify each Stripe price maps to product and plan before paid-product activation.Billing events grant or suspend the correct access.Money / access control[verified]
Logging and auditReview monitoring, logs, errors, email events, auth events, billing events, and client events.Support can explain login, billing, email, activation, and tenant-boundary outcomes.Audit[verified]
Backup and restoreRun documented backup/restore scripts and validate login, licenses, billing events, email outbox, and /ready.Recovery preserves customer-facing license and account data.Data retention / recovery[verified]
Regulated/CUI handlingDefine whether customer, billing, license, device, or audit data falls under CUI or another regulated-data scope.Support handling, export, deletion, and retention rules become clear.Regulated/CUI data[unknown — needs SME input]

Governance Review Workflow

Precondition: A product, environment, release, or support process is being prepared for staging acceptance, production promotion, or periodic audit.

  1. [verified] Confirm named admin accounts, role-based route protection, customer tenant boundaries, and least-privilege GCP permissions.
  2. [verified] Confirm development credentials, development license-creation routes, placeholder secrets, SQLite, insecure cookies, and wildcard hosts are absent from staging or production.
  3. [verified] Confirm product client APIs reject missing or invalid X-TSG-Product-ID and X-TSG-API-Key headers.
  4. [verified] Confirm password setup/reset tokens expire and are single-use.
  5. [verified] Confirm Stripe signature verification uses the exact HTTP request body and rejects tampered payloads.
  6. [verified] Confirm SMTP credentials are stored outside source control and staging/production does not depend on database-stored SMTP passwords.
  7. [verified] Confirm backups are protected and restore validation includes login, licenses, billing events, email outbox, and /ready.
  8. [unknown — needs SME input] Define and record regulated/CUI handling rules for support screenshots, exports, logs, backups, retention, and deletion requests.
  9. [verified] Record security, operations, product-owner, and release-manager signoffs.

Expected result: Governance checks are explicit, auditable, and tied to product/customer access risks.

How to confirm success: Security checklist items are signed off or documented as open environment-evidence gaps before release acceptance.

Lifecycle & Decision Points

Confidence Summary: Activation, billing, password reset, deployment, rollback, and support branches are mostly [verified]; scale/retire ownership and regulated-data decisions are [inferred — confirm] or [unknown — needs SME input] where repository docs do not define business policy.

End-User Track Decision Map

Decision pointBranch conditionActionBasis
Use setup link vs. request a new linkSetup link expired or already usedRequest a new setup/reset link and use the newest email only.[verified]
Sign in vs. reset passwordPassword is forgotten or rejectedUse password reset when available; contact support if account state is unclear.[verified]
Activate vs. contact supportActivation succeeds, or activation is deniedCheck product key, internet access, license status, and device limit before contacting support.[verified]
Deactivate vs. keep a deviceDevice limit reached or old workstation no longer neededDeactivate an old device only after confirming access is no longer needed there.[verified]
Resolve billing vs. waitSubscription-backed access is suspended after failed paymentResolve payment issue or contact billing support.[verified]
Use current portal vs. switch portalPortal shows the wrong product contextSign out and open the correct product portal URL.[verified]

Admin Track Decision Map

Decision pointBranch conditionActionBasis
Activate product vs. holdProduct, tenant, plan, Stripe mapping, entitlement enforcement, or portal evidence is incompleteKeep product inactive until the missing acceptance check is complete.[verified]
Provision reset token vs. deny requestCustomer identity and email/license relationship are verified, or not verifiedRegenerate setup/reset token only for a verified customer.[verified]
Deactivate device vs. preserve accessCustomer requested/approved deactivation, or approval is missingDeactivate only when requested or approved; otherwise preserve access and escalate.[verified]
Grant paid access vs. blockStripe signed event is valid, tamper rejection passes, and replay is idempotent, or any check failsGrant/update access only when webhook validation passes.[verified]
Continue release vs. rollback/ready, tenant isolation, activation, Stripe replay, or email smoke tests failShift to rollback rehearsal or previous revision and rerun smoke tests.[verified]
Rotate credentials vs. continueProduct API key is exposed or suspected exposedRotate product API keys and distribute through an approved secure channel.[verified]
Scale up vs. hold capacityMetrics or runtime evidence show sustained load or errorsUse platform operations policy to decide scaling or capacity changes.[inferred — confirm]
Retain/export/delete regulated recordsCustomer data is confirmed as CUI or otherwise regulatedApply approved regulated-data retention, export, and deletion rules.[unknown — needs SME input]

Troubleshooting

Confidence Summary: End-user failure modes are [verified] from the customer guide; admin failure modes are [verified] from the runbook, admin guide, deployment guide, and security checklist, with one regulated-data escalation gap marked [unknown — needs SME input].

User-Resolvable Issues

SymptomLikely causeActionBasis
Setup link expiredToken expired or was already usedRequest a new setup/reset link and use the newest email only.[verified]
Cannot sign inWrong portal, wrong email, wrong password, or inactive accountConfirm portal URL, email, and password; then use password reset.[verified]
License missingLicense assigned to another email or product contextConfirm the license was assigned to the same email used to sign in.[verified]
Activation deniedProduct key, internet access, license status, or device limit issueCheck product key, internet access, license status, and device limit.[verified]
Device limit reachedToo many active devicesDeactivate an old device or contact support.[verified]
Product feature missingPlan does not include the module or extensionConfirm your plan includes the module or extension.[verified]
Billing suspensionPayment issue affected subscription-backed accessResolve payment issue or contact billing support.[verified]
Portal shows wrong productIncorrect portal URL or stale sessionSign out and open the correct product portal URL.[verified]

Admin-Only Issues

SymptomLikely causeActionBasis
/ready failsDatabase connectivity, migration, or PostgreSQL health problemCheck PostgreSQL service health, connection string, backup status, and migration version.[verified]
/health failsRuntime or deployment problemReview Cloud Run revision, image digest, runtime errors, and deployment logs.[verified]
Stripe webhook failuresSignature, mapping, tamper, replay, or delivery issueCheck /admin/billing-events, /admin/errors, and Stripe event delivery logs.[verified]
Email delivery failuresSMTP setting, provider credential, template, or routing issueCheck /admin/email-events, SMTP settings, and product portal/base URL links.[verified]
Cross-tenant license visibilityTenant boundary or portal routing defectStop release acceptance and verify product portal routing, tenant isolation, and customer/product boundaries.[verified]
Product API activation failsMissing or invalid product headers or API keyVerify X-TSG-Product-ID, X-TSG-API-Key, product ID, API base URL, and credential status.[verified]
Production startup blockedUnsafe secrets, SQLite, missing Stripe config, insecure cookies, or wildcard hostsFix the unsafe configuration and rerun startup diagnostics before public use.[verified]
Regulated/CUI handling unclearNo approved product-specific data classification existsEscalate to the compliance owner before exporting, retaining, deleting, or sharing regulated records.[unknown — needs SME input]

Verification Checklist

Confidence Summary: Checklist items are [verified] where they repeat acceptance, customer, admin, security, deployment, and backup evidence; regulated/CUI verification remains [unknown — needs SME input].

End-User Track

  • [verified] Portal URL matches the expected product.
  • [verified] Password setup or password reset completed without forwarding the link or sharing the password.
  • [verified] Sign-in succeeds with the licensed account email.
  • [verified] Dashboard or license area shows the expected product, plan, status, device limit, and active devices.
  • [verified] Product activation succeeds only with the correct product key and allowed device.
  • [verified] Old devices are deactivated only after confirming access is no longer needed there.
  • [verified] Downloads, updates, extensions, billing, and organization/team details are reviewed where enabled.
  • [verified] Support request excludes passwords, full payment card details, API keys, private credentials, and configuration secrets.

Admin Track

  • [verified] Named admin accounts, approved roles, tenant boundaries, and product portal routing are verified.
  • [verified] Required secrets are stored outside source control in Secret Manager or an approved secret store.
  • [verified] Staging/production rejects placeholders, weak secrets, SQLite, missing Stripe configuration, insecure cookies, and wildcard hosts.
  • [verified] /health, /ready, /metrics, Admin Monitoring, Billing Events, and Email Events are reviewed.
  • [verified] Product client APIs reject missing or invalid product credentials.
  • [verified] Activation, refresh, deactivation, and device-limit tests pass.
  • [verified] Stripe signed payload, tamper rejection, and replay idempotency pass before paid plans are activated.
  • [verified] SMTP staging delivery passes and links route to the correct product portal/base URL.
  • [verified] Backup/restore and rollback evidence includes login, licenses, billing events, email outbox, /ready, tenant isolation, activation, and Stripe replay checks.
  • [unknown — needs SME input] Regulated/CUI data classification, retention, export, deletion, backup protection, and support-handling rules are approved and recorded.
  • [verified] Security, operations, product-owner, and release-manager signoffs are recorded.
